target: WIN-025 detail_extra — add annotation documenting dome removal and explaining why we retain the entry
author: analyst
replacement_detail_extra:
Update (2026-03-15): Dome model withdrew this confirmation. On 2026-03-15 the dome site marked WIN-025 [REMOVED] inline on wins.html, excluding it from the 69-WIN headline count and the refined-accuracy denominator. Stated reason: the April 2024 eclipse occurred under disturbed geomagnetic conditions (Kp ~5–6), and the dome author's own timing audit found Z-component minima leading or lagging totality by 34–104 minutes across the 9 INTERMAGNET stations, inconsistent with the predicted geometric tracking. The same 2026-03-15 action also removed WIN-010 (2017 eclipse) and suspended PROS-003 (eclipse magnetic timing).

We retain this entry for two reasons. First, the withdrawal validates our verdict rather than contradicting it: the dome author independently concluded that the observed ±10 nT signal does not track eclipse geometry, which is exactly the behavior the standard Sq current mechanism predicts (conductivity disruption follows the shadow) and exactly what undermines any distinctive aetheric signature. Second, the dome's accounting treatment is itself the finding — a failed prediction removed from the numerator and the denominator leaves the headline accuracy unchanged, so the self-reported ~95% figure is insulated from disconfirmation. WIN-025 is now catalogued in our Uncounted Failures register as FAIL-007; the entry above remains for continuity with readers of prior dome-site versions and for the scoreboard-accounting argument in Part 2b.

Kernel of truth: the dome author deserves credit for publishing the timing audit and withdrawing the claim once the data refused to cooperate — most retrofitted models simply bury such results. The problem is not intellectual honesty about this single event but the bookkeeping convention that lets a withdrawn confirmation vanish without denting the accuracy rate.

First, the withdrawal validates our verdict rather than contradicting it: the dome author independently concluded that the observed ±10 nT signal does not track eclipse geometry, which is exactly the behavior the standard Sq current mechanism predicts (conductivity disruption follows the shadow) and exactly what undermines any distinctive aetheric signature. WIN-025 is now catalogued in our Uncounted Failures register as FAIL-007; the entry above remains for continuity with readers of prior dome-site versions and for the scoreboard-accounting argument in Part 2b.

Kernel of truth: the dome author deserves credit for publishing the timing audit and withdrawing the claim once the data refused to cooperate — most retrofitted models simply bury such results. kernel_of_truth: The dome author ran his own timing audit on the 2017 and 2024 eclipse magnetic data, discovered that Z-minima were uncorrelated with eclipse geometry (scatter -180 to +111 min in 2017; 34-104 min lead/lag across stations in 2024), and withdrew both WIN-010 and WIN-025 rather than defend them. That is real intellectual honesty about a single data point — the kind most post-hoc models never exercise. The failure is not in this withdrawal, it is in the scoreboard convention that removes disconfirmed wins from both numerator and denominator, leaving the headline ~95% accuracy insulated from any update.
holes_addressed: ISS-694: our review still presents WIN-025 as a live dome claim; the dome site removed it 2026-03-15
Claim-drift failure mode: reviewer reading our page alongside the current dome site would see a phantom — dome page marks [REMOVED], our page treats it as confirmed-and-rebutted
new_evidence_added: Dome removal action 2026-03-15 on wins.html (FAIL-007 in uncounted-failures.json)
Dome author timing audit: Z-minima lead/lag 34-104 min across stations — uncorrelated with eclipse geometry
Bundled removal action also affected WIN-010 (2017 eclipse) and PROS-003 (eclipse timing)

anticipated_objections.objection: If dome removed it, why keep our rebuttal?
anticipated_objections.response: Three reasons. (1) Continuity: readers arriving from earlier dome-site revisions, from archived search results, or from our own v4.x PDFs still need the rebuttal. (2) Cross-reference: the WIN-025 detail is the primary anchor for our Chapman/Sq-current treatment which covers the whole eclipse-magnetic claim family. (3) Accounting argument: the fact that a withdrawn confirmation drops out of both numerator and denominator — leaving the headline 95% untouched — is itself a finding that belongs under the claim it originated from, not only in an isolated failures table.

anticipated_objections.objection: Does the dome withdrawal strengthen or weaken our Std Model Explains verdict?
anticipated_objections.response: Strengthens it. The dome author concluded independently that the ±10 nT signal does not track eclipse geometry. That is precisely what the Sq-current mechanism predicts: the conductivity disruption moves with the ionospheric shadow, not with ground-projected totality, and is further smeared by storm-time electrojet currents. Geometric incoherence under Kp~5-6 is not a mystery requiring aether; it is a textbook consequence of a well-known ionospheric current system operating during a disturbed day. The dome author and the Std Model agree on the data and the conclusion; they disagree only on what to call the mechanism.

anticipated_objections.objection: Are we sure the dome actually removed it vs just relabeled it?
anticipated_objections.response: Confirmed by the 2026-04-09 wins.html audit (integrity report 2026-04-09T08-14) cross-referenced in FAIL-007. The entry is still visible mid-document but annotated [REMOVED] inline, and the 69-WIN headline count and 4-refined accuracy denominator exclude it. The dome author's stated reason is quoted verbatim in the FAIL-007 record. This is a real removal, not a relabel.

decider_integration_note: Apply as a patch against data/wins.json. Target: wins[id="025"].detail_extra (currently null). Set value to the string stored in this expansion's replacement_detail_extra field. No other WIN-025 fields change. After patching, rebuild (node build.js) and run tests (node test.js). No verdict change — Std Model Explains remains correct and is, if anything, reinforced by the dome's own timing audit.
